draft.introduction: Proverbs 3 contains the famous exhortation to “trust in the Lord with all your heart” and presents Wisdom as God’s agent in creation. It is among the most memorized chapters in the wisdom literature.

translation.text: Trust in the Lord with all your heart, and do not lean on your own understanding. In all your ways acknowledge him, and he will make straight your paths. The Lord by wisdom founded the earth; by understanding he established the heavens; by his knowledge the deeps broke open.
translation.original_text: ἴσθι πεποιθὼς ἐν ὅλῃ καρδίᾳ ἐπὶ θεῷ

translator_notes.note: The description of Wisdom as God’s instrument in creation anticipates the Logos theology of John 1. The Church Fathers debated whether Proverbs’ personified Wisdom was a literary device or a genuine hypostasis—a question that fed directly into Trinitarian theology.
